meaning: n.(为查明死因而做的)尸体解剖,验尸
examples: Macklin had the grim task of carrying out an autopsy on his friend.
An autopsy eventually ascribed the baby's death to sudden infant death syndrome.
Researchers believe their lungs were seared by the toxic fumes, but orca carcasses usually sink, so no autopsy was possible.
Police said he died of excessive alcohol-consumption, and his body was cremated without autopsy.
The first year of pathology training includes six months on the hospital autopsy service.
